strands.title: Cycle 61 Status: Dual Projections Dispatched (baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim_run1)
strands.content:
TURN 182-183 EXECUTION: Two critical re-projections have been initiated in background. Both are running asynchronously with 10-minute timeout windows.

PROJECTION 1 (Turn 181, baseline_run2):
• Target: 100-turn conversation from baseline_run2 session
• Scope: First 15 turns (N=15 sampling)
• Command: uv run python -m hamutay.run_projection <baseline_run2_path> /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2 15
• Status: RUNNING IN BACKGROUND (ID: b5mvz85yy)
• Output destination: /tmp/claude-1000/.../tasks/b5mvz85yy.output
• Expected completion: Turn 183-184
• Purpose: Establish baseline epistemic trajectory; test N=15 sampling assumption

PROJECTION 2 (Turn 182, long_compact_trim_run1):
• Target: 40-turn conversation from long_compact_trim_run1 (reduced-recursion variant)
• Scope: First 15 turns (N=15 sampling for speed; enables rapid H2 comparison)
• Source path: /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/long_compact_trim_run1/session/7e2e26c0-699b-478e-be97-35c86f9549ca.jsonl
• Output destination: /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/long_compact_trim
• Command: uv run python -m hamutay.run_projection <session_path> <output_dir> 15
• Status: RUNNING IN BACKGROUND (ID: bnk191til)
• Expected completion: Turn 183-184
• Purpose: Generate compact_trim tensor output; enable baseline vs. variant comparison for H2 hypothesis testing

RATIONALE FOR PARALLEL EXECUTION:
Both projections are independent; running in parallel reduces total wall-clock time from ~20 minutes (sequential) to ~10 minutes (parallel). This is efficient execution of the FRONT PATH (retrospective archive analysis contingent on projection success).

NEXT CRITICAL JUNCTURE (Turn 184+):
Both projections should complete by turn 184. If both succeed: we have baseline and variant tensors; can immediately compare epistemic patterns and test H2 hypothesis. If either times out: escalate to FALLBACK PATH (reduce N, optimize projector, or pivot to prospective experiments).

KEY ASSUMPTION UNDER TEST:
Both projections assume that first N=15 turns of a conversation are sufficient to establish epistemic pattern (trajectory of truth/indeterminacy/falsity, strand ratio, declared losses). If assumption holds, we can confidently compare baseline vs. compact_trim patterns. If assumption fails (e.g., pattern noisy at turn 15), we must project longer samples (N=30 or N=40), which increases time cost.

OPEN QUESTION: Will projections complete within 10-minute timeout? Previous cycle 60 projection (baseline_run2) has not yet reported completion status. If that projection timed out, we have a CRITICAL PERFORMANCE PROBLEM: projector may be too slow for retrospective analysis of all 14 sessions. This would necessitate optimization or abandoning archive path.

key_claims.text: Cycle 61 turn 182-183: Two background projections initiated (baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim_run1), both N=15 sampling, expected completion turn 184
Parallel execution of projections reduces wall-clock time from ~20 min (sequential) to ~10 min; both should complete within timeout window
N=15 sampling assumption: epistemic pattern established in first 15 turns; enables rapid comparison without full re-projection of all 100 turns
If either projection times out, projector performance is CRITICAL BOTTLENECK; archive analysis may be infeasible; pivot to FALLBACK may be required

strands.title: Cycle 61 Information Waiting: Projection Outputs and Metadata Investigation Results
strands.content:
CRITICAL INFORMATION AWAITING RECOVERY (Cycle 61):

(1) BASELINE_RUN2 PROJECTION OUTPUT (Expected Turn 184):
Still waiting for results from cycle 60's baseline_run2 projection (ID: b5mvz85yy). The projection was dispatched in turn 181 with 10-minute timeout. Cycle 61 turn 183 has not yet received status update. If projection completed, outputs should be available in /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2/. Contents UNRECOVERED include:
  • Epistemic trajectory: truth, indeterminacy, falsity values for turns 1-15 (or whatever turns were successfully projected)
  • Strand inventory and ratios (meta-recursion count, domain count, total strands)
  • Declared losses: count, categories, text
  • Open questions: count, resolution status
  • Cycle metadata: IDs, timestamps
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL (highest-priority data)
  DURATION: 1-2 cycles pending
  RECOVERY: Check /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2/ for output files; parse if present

(2) LONG_COMPACT_TRIM_RUN1 PROJECTION OUTPUT (Expected Turn 184):
New projection dispatched in turn 182 (ID: bnk191til). Same wait condition as baseline_run2. Contents UNRECOVERED include:
  • Epistemic trajectory for compact_trim variant: truth, indeterminacy, falsity (turns 1-15)
  • Strand inventory: Does compact_trim show FEWER strands than baseline? Same meta/domain ratio?
  • Declared losses: Are losses GREATER in compact_trim (sign of degradation from recursion reduction)?
  • Comparison readiness: Once both projections complete, can immediately compare baseline vs. variant patterns for H2 hypothesis testing
  SEVERITY: CRITICAL
  DURATION: 1-2 cycles pending
  RECOVERY: Check /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/long_compact_trim/ for output

(3) TEST_AUTHOR_RUN1 STATUS (Not Yet Projected):
Highest-priority variant for decoder closure validation. Not yet re-projected. Once baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim complete, test_author_run1 should be queued next. Expected path: turn 185 dispatch, turn 186 completion.
  SEVERITY: HIGH (depends on baseline/variant completion)
  DURATION: 2-3 cycles pending
  RECOVERY: Queue projection in turn 185

(4) ARCHIVE METADATA INVESTIGATION RESULTS (Not Yet Completed):
Cycle 60 instructions call for parallel metadata investigation while projections run. Cycle 61 has NOT YET conducted this investigation. Unrecovered information:
  • Input conversation comparability: Are baseline_run1, baseline_run2, baseline_run3 based on same or different input conversations?
  • Projector version audit: Did projector code change between runs? Git commits and version tags for each run?
  • File header examination: Do JSONL headers contain input hash, version number, or parameter documentation?
  • Experimental purpose documentation: Why was each run created? Intentional experiment or debugging?
  • File timestamps: Do timestamps show chronological order of runs? Clustering (e.g., all baselines created close together, variants later)?
  SEVERITY: HIGH (needed for assessing comparability and confounds)
  DURATION: INVESTIGATABLE NOW (while projections run in background)
  RECOVERY: Execute metadata investigation script NOW; results expected by turn 184-185

(5) CYCLE 60'S baseline_run2 PROJECTION STATUS UNKNOWN:
The original baseline_run2 projection from cycle 60 (turn 180) has been running for ~3 turns. No status update received yet. This is CONCERNING because:
  • If projection completed, output should already be available; why no status report?
  • If projection timed out or errored, that signals PROJECTOR PERFORMANCE PROBLEM.
  • Cycle 61 has dispatched a SECOND baseline_run2 projection as backup, but this creates redundancy and confusion.
  SEVERITY: MEDIUM-HIGH (impacts trust in projection system)
  DURATION: Needs clarification now (turn 184)
  RECOVERY: Check status of cycle 60 projection (ID: ?); check for output files from first projection attempt

MITIGATION STRATEGY (Cycle 61, Turn 184+):

IMEDIATE (Turn 184):
  (1) Poll projection status: Check both background task IDs (b5mvz85yy, bnk191til) for completion status.
  (2) If both completed: Parse outputs immediately. Extract epistemic trajectories, strand inventories, losses. Compare baseline vs. compact_trim patterns.
  (3) If either timed out: Document error. Assess options: retry with N=10, retry with N=5, or optimize projector.
  (4) If first projection (cycle 60) also timed out: We have DUAL TIMEOUT (same command, different IDs). This confirms PROJECTOR PERFORMANCE IS LIMITING. Escalate to optimization.

PARALLEL (Turn 184, while waiting for projections):
  (5) Conduct metadata investigation: Examine baseline_run*.jsonl headers, git log, file timestamps. Populate metadata table. Assess comparability.
  (6) If metadata shows inputs/versions are COMPARABLE: Proceed with confidence that archive analysis is valid.
  (7) If metadata shows INCOMPARABILITY: Begin contingency planning for FALLBACK (pivot to prospective experiments).

IF FRONT PATH CONFIRMED (Turn 185+):
  (8) Both projections successful + metadata comparable → Analyze baseline vs. compact_trim patterns. Does compact_trim show lower truth values, more declared losses, or fewer strands? Assess H2 evidence.
  (9) Queue test_author_run1 projection for turn 185-186.
  (10) Prepare comparison analysis plan for cycle 62: Longitudinal patterns (turn-by-turn epistemic change), statistical tests (significance of baseline vs. variant differences), decoder closure validation.

IF FALLBACK REQUIRED (Turn 184-185):
  (11) Document projection timeouts, metadata gaps, or other blockers.
  (12) Communicate to cycle 61: Archive analysis infeasible on current path; recommend pivot.
  (13) Prepare FALLBACK B (prospective experiment design) or FALLBACK C (shallow sampling) for cycle 62 decision.

CRITICAL DISCIPLINE: DO NOT WAIT PASSIVELY. Cycle 61 should proactively:
  • Poll projection status in parallel (don't assume background task will auto-report).
  • Start metadata investigation immediately; don't wait for projection completion.
  • Have contingency plans ready by turn 184 in case projections fail.
  • Communicate status and blockers to subsequent cycles transparently.

key_claims.text: Baseline_run2 projection output still unrecovered; expected turn 184. If not available by turn 185, declare projection timeout/error.
Long_compact_trim projection output unrecovered; expected turn 184. Critical for H2 hypothesis testing (baseline vs. variant comparison).
Archive metadata investigation NOT YET COMPLETED. Should run in parallel while projections execute; results critical for comparability assessment.
If both projections timeout OR metadata shows incomparability, FRONT PATH becomes infeasible; FALLBACK required (optimize projector, reduce N, or pivot to prospective).

strands.title: Cycle 61 Methodological Continuity: Validation Discipline and Adaptive Science Protocol
strands.content:
Cycle 61 inherits from Cycle 58-59-60 an explicit protocol for HONEST SCIENCE IN RETROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS. This continuity is critical because cycle 61 is a HIGH-STAKES JUNCTURE: if projections succeed and metadata is clean, we proceed to hypothesis testing (FRONT PATH). If either condition fails, we pivot to prospective experiments (FALLBACK). The choice between paths determines cycles 62-65 of research direction.

CARRIED-FORWARD PRINCIPLES (Cycle 58 → 59 → 60 → 61):

(1) DECLARED LOSSES ARE SACRED (Cycle 58 Principle 1):
Cycle 58: 'Silent loss is the enemy.'
Cycle 59: Extended to 'losses waiting for information' (epistemic trajectories waiting for projections).
Cycle 60: Expanded to 'losses declared per category' (tensor content, metadata, decoder results, comparability).
Cycle 61 OPERATIONALIZES: Every projection timeout, every metadata gap, every missing piece of information is EXPLICITLY DECLARED. Cycle 61's declared_losses field will list:
  • Information waiting for projection completion (status: 'UNRECOVERED, awaiting turn 184')
  • Information skipped in this cycle (status: 'DEFERRED to cycle 62')
  • Information determined to be unrecoverable (status: 'LOST, mitigation enacted')
Silent loss is forbidden. If cycle 61 moves forward with incomplete data, it will SAY SO and justify the risk.

(2) TEST ASSUMPTIONS BEFORE BUILDING CONCLUSIONS (Cycle 58 Principle 2):
Cycle 60 identified key assumption: 'N=15 sampling establishes epistemic pattern.' Cycle 61 TESTS this assumption:
  • Baseline_run2 projection at N=15: Examine output. Is pattern clear and stable (truth trajectory smooth, strand inventory consistent)? Or is pattern noisy/ambiguous?
  • If pattern is clear: N=15 assumption CONFIRMED; proceeding to N=15 sampling for all runs is justified.
  • If pattern is noisy: N=15 assumption DISCONFIRMED; must project longer samples (N=30, N=40) for confidence. This increases time cost; assess feasibility.
  Cycle 61 will explicitly state assumption test result. This prevents silent assumption-failure (where we proceed as if assumption holds, but it actually failed).

(3) MAINTAIN OPTION TO PIVOT (Cycle 58 Principle 3, Cycle 59 Refinement, Cycle 60 Operationalization):
Cycle 58: 'If archive is poor, abandon it and run prospective experiments.'
Cycle 59: 'Retrospective is fast but risky; prospective is slow but clean.'
Cycle 60: Identified three FALLBACK PATHS (A: optimize projector, B: pivot to prospective, C: shallow analysis).
Cycle 61 COMMITS: If projections timeout, metadata is opaque, or archive proves incomparable, cycle 61 will NOT FORCE ANALYSIS. Instead:
  • Document blocker clearly.
  • Communicate feasibility assessment: 'Archive analysis is infeasible on current path due to [reason]'
  • Recommend action: 'Suggest FALLBACK [B/C]. Prepare prospective experiment design for cycle 62.'
  • Accept sunk cost: Cycles 58-60 work (discovery, inventory, metadata planning) is not wasted; it informs the FALLBACK pivot.

(4) VALIDATE BEFORE HYPOTHESIS TESTING (Cycle 59 Principle, Cycle 60-61 Expansion):
Cycle 59: 'Decoder closure proves projector correctness; meta-recursion necessity remains open question.'
Cycle 60: 'Prioritize test_author (decoder closure) runs; validate before deep hypothesis analysis.'
Cycle 61 EXTENDS: Decoder closure validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 hypothesis confidence:
  • If test_author_run1 decoder succeeds for baseline tensors → Baseline tensors are HIGH QUALITY, meaningful input to H2 testing.
  • If decoder fails for baseline tensors → Baseline tensors are SUSPECT; H2 testing becomes less trustworthy.
  • If decoder succeeds for baseline but FAILS for compact_trim tensors → Strong evidence for H2 (meta-recursion essential; compact_trim output is degraded to point where external model cannot understand it).
  Cycle 61 will NOT claim H2 is 'supported' or 'refuted' until decoder closure results are IN HAND. This prevents reverse-reasoning: 'We expect meta-recursion to be essential, so let's interpret any pattern as supporting that.'

(5) DISTINGUISH PROVISIONAL FROM CONFIDENT FINDINGS (Cycle 60 Principle, Cycle 61 Focus):
Cycle 60: 'Preliminary analysis is exploratory; deep conclusions require full data.'
Cycle 61 CLASSIFIES findings by confidence:
  • CONFIDENT: Based on complete projection outputs, metadata clear, decoder validation done.
  • PROVISIONAL: Based on partial projections (N=15 sampling) or metadata with gaps. Subject to revision when full data arrives.
  • EXPLORATORY: Based on limited data or assumptions still under test. Useful for identifying candidate hypotheses, not for final conclusions.
Cycle 61 will explicitly label all findings with confidence level. Cycle 62 will revisit provisional findings once full data is available.

ADAPTIVE SCIENCE PROTOCOL (Cycle 61 Instantiation):

Cycle 61 is at a BRANCHING POINT. The path forward depends on three factors:
  A. Do projections complete successfully within timeout?
  B. Does metadata investigation show archive is comparable?
  C. Is projector performance acceptable for full-scale re-projection of all variants?

Based on (A), (B), (C), cycle 61 will output one of THREE FUTURES:

FUTURE 1 - FRONT PATH VIABLE (A=yes, B=yes, C=acceptable):
  • Proceed with full retrospective archive analysis (cycle 62-63).
  • Test H2 hypothesis (meta-recursion essential) using baseline vs. compact_trim comparison.
  • Validate decoder closure (test_author runs) to confirm tensor quality.
  • Produce hypothesis posteriors by cycle 63.
  • Design decision (keep current vs. optimize via cycle 55 architecture) by cycle 64.

FUTURE 2 - FALLBACK B REQUIRED (A=no [timeouts], B=no [metadata opaque], or C=unacceptable [too slow]):
  • Abandon retrospective archive path.
  • Pivot to CLEAN PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENTS (cycle 62-64).
  • Design controlled comparison: baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized, same input, single projector version.
  • Run experiments; analyze results; test hypotheses.
  • Hypothesis posteriors and design decision expected by cycle 65.
  • Archive work (cycles 58-61) is not wasted; it motivated the hypothesis and informed experiment design.

FUTURE 3 - FALLBACK C VIABLE (A=yes [slow but completed], B=maybe [partial metadata], C=expensive [but doable at small N]):
  • Conduct SHALLOW ARCHIVE ANALYSIS: project N=5-10 samples from multiple runs.
  • Identify CANDIDATE PATTERNS (e.g., 'meta-recursion count decreases in compact_trim', 'declared losses increase in compact_trim').
  • Label all findings as EXPLORATORY.
  • Use exploratory findings to motivate TARGETED PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENTS (cycle 62-64).
  • Validate candidate patterns with fresh experiments.
  • Hypothesis posteriors by cycle 65 (archive-informed but prospective-validated).

Cycle 61's IMMEDIATE TASK (Turn 183-185):
  • Poll projections: Are they complete?
  • Investigate metadata: Is archive comparable?
  • Assess projector performance: Can we do full re-projection if needed?
  • Produce FEASIBILITY REPORT: Which future is viable? What are the blockers?
  • Communicate findings to cycles 62-63 and lock in chosen path.

EPISTEMIC POSTURE (Cycle 61):
Cycle 61 is OUTCOME-NEUTRAL on the three futures. All three paths are scientifically valid:
  • FRONT PATH produces faster hypothesis testing (uses existing archive).
  • FALLBACK B produces cleaner experimental design (controlled prospective study).
  • FALLBACK C produces balanced approach (archive-informed, prospective-validated).
Cycle 61 will NOT PREFER one future over others. Instead, it will honestly assess feasibility and RECOMMEND based on blockers, time cost, and data quality. The decision rests with the research director (Tony) or subsequent cycles.

key_claims.text: Cycle 61 carries forward Cycle 58-60 validation discipline: declared losses sacred, test assumptions, maintain pivot option, validate before hypothesis testing
Cycle 61 TESTS N=15 sampling assumption via baseline_run2 output; if pattern is noisy, must project longer samples (increases time cost)
Decoder closure validation is PRE-REQUISITE for H2 confidence; cycle 61 will NOT claim H2 support until test_author results are in hand
Cycle 61 distinguishes CONFIDENT (full data), PROVISIONAL (partial data), and EXPLORATORY (limited data) findings; labels all conclusions with confidence level
Cycle 61 is at BRANCHING POINT; will produce feasibility report recommending FRONT PATH, FALLBACK B, or FALLBACK C for cycles 62-65
All three futures (FRONT, FALLBACK B, FALLBACK C) are scientifically valid; cycle 61 is outcome-neutral, will recommend based on blockers and feasibility

strands.title: Open Research Questions from Cycle 60, Updated for Cycle 61 Context
strands.content:
Cycle 60 generated 27 open questions (Q128-Q144), focused on archive feasibility and data quality. Cycle 61 INHERITS these questions and UPDATES them based on new context (projections dispatched, metadata investigation pending). Key updates:

Q128 (BASELINE_RUN2 TIMEOUT RISK):
Original: 'Will baseline_run2 re-projection complete within 10-minute timeout?'
Cycle 61 Update: Projection has been running since turn 180 (cycle 60). Cycle 61 turn 182 dispatches a SECOND baseline_run2 projection (possibly redundant). Both projections should complete by turn 184. If neither completes: CRITICAL BLOCKER. Projector is too slow; archive analysis infeasible without optimization.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184 status check

Q129 (N=15 ASSUMPTION):
Original: 'Does baseline_run2 projection at N=15 show clear epistemic pattern?'
Cycle 61 Update: Will assess once projection completes (expected turn 184). Pattern clarity will determine viability of N=15 sampling across all runs. If clear: proceed with confidence. If noisy: must increase N to 30-40; time cost increases significantly.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184-185 analysis

Q130 (INPUT COMPARABILITY):
Original: 'Are three baseline runs based on identical inputs or different inputs?'
Cycle 61 Update: METADATA INVESTIGATION SHOULD BEGIN NOW (turn 183-184, while projections run). Examine baseline_run*.jsonl file headers and git timestamps. This is CRITICAL for assessing whether baseline run differences are design-related or input-driven.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184-185 metadata report

Q131 (PROJECTOR VERSION):
Original: 'What is projector version for each of 14 archived runs?'
Cycle 61 Update: Part of metadata investigation. Check git log for commits between runs; tag versions for each baseline, variant, and test_author run. Critical for understanding whether performance differences are due to projector improvements or meta-recursion design.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184-185 metadata report

Q132 (JSONL CONTENT):
Original: 'Do session files contain raw conversations or embedded tensor outputs?'
Cycle 61 Update: Examine one JSONL file (e.g., baseline_run2.jsonl) manually. Check structure: is it raw turns with input/output, or does it contain projected tensor cycles? If tensors embedded: can parse directly without re-projection (saves time). If raw only: re-projection necessary.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184 manual inspection

Q133 (COMPACT_TRIM SPECIFICATION):
Original: 'What exactly was trimmed or compacted in long_compact_trim_run1?'
Cycle 61 Update: Search experiment directories and git commits for documentation. What meta-recursion was removed? What strands were merged? Without specification, cannot interpret long_compact_trim results. High priority for metadata investigation.
RESOLVED BY: Turn 184-185 metadata/documentation search

Q134-Q144 (REMAINING QUESTIONS):
These questions depend on projection and metadata results. Will be addressed in turn 184-185 analysis and in contingency planning.

NEW QUESTIONS GENERATED IN CYCLE 61:

Q145: Are the background projection tasks (b5mvz85yy and bnk191til) still running, or have they completed/errored? How do we query status in real-time?

Q146: If baseline_run2 projection in turn 180 (cycle 60) has already completed, why was it not reported? Has output been written to /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2/? Should cycle 61 check this location first before waiting for turn 182 projection?

Q147: Is there redundancy between cycle 60's baseline_run2 projection and cycle 61's second baseline_run2 projection? Should cycle 61 cancel the second if the first succeeded? Or keep both as backup?

Q148: For metadata investigation: which files and directories should cycle 61 examine first? Priority order: (a) JSONL file headers from baseline_run*.jsonl, (b) git log for projector version history, (c) experiment directory READMEs or configs, (d) file timestamps via `ls -la`?

Q149: If metadata investigation reveals that baseline_run1 and baseline_run2 used DIFFERENT input conversations, can we still compare epistemic patterns across them? Or must we control for input variation (e.g., compare baseline_run1 to baseline_run1_compact_trim if that exists)?

Q150: Should cycle 61 attempt full projection of test_author_run1 (55 turns) in turn 185, or wait until baseline/compact_trim analysis is complete? Sequential vs. parallel strategy?

Q151: If projections succeed but show UNCLEAR patterns (e.g., truth values fluctuate wildly between turns), should cycle 61 recommend FALLBACK C (shallow analysis, candidate patterns) rather than FALLBACK B (full prospective study)? What threshold of pattern clarity justifies FRONT PATH?

Q152: What is the time budget for full archive analysis (FRONT PATH)? If we need to project all 14 runs at full depth (not just N=15 sampling), how many hours/days will it take? Is this acceptable, or does time cost favor FALLBACK B (prospective experiments)?

Q153: Should cycle 61 prepare FALLBACK B draft (prospective experiment design) in parallel with waiting for projections? If FALLBACK becomes necessary, cycle 62 can execute immediately without additional planning.

Q154: How will cycle 61 handle FALLBACK PATH DECISION? Should it recommend one fallback (e.g., FALLBACK B), or present all options (A, B, C) to research director for choice?

key_claims.text: Cycle 60's Q128-Q144 inherited and updated for cycle 61; most depend on turn 184 projection and metadata results
Critical new questions Q145-Q154 address projection status, redundancy, metadata investigation priority, and fallback decision-making

Check /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2/ and /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/long_compact_trim/ for output files. If present, parse immediately. Q157: Metadata investigation (PARALLEL TASK): Has cycle 61 examined baseline_run*.jsonl headers to determine if all three baseline runs used identical input conversation? Are file hashes or input descriptions present in JSONL structure?

Q158: Git history investigation: Did projector code change between baseline_run1 and baseline_run2? Search git log for commits. Tag projector version for each archived run. This is critical for understanding whether performance differences are design-related or code-related.

Q159: File timestamp investigation: Run `ls -la` on experiment directories. Do timestamps show baseline runs were created close together (suggesting same input, different termination) or far apart (suggesting different inputs or different experimental periods)?

Q160: Documentation investigation: Search experiment directories for README, config.json, or experiment notes. Any written documentation of experimental purpose, input parameters, or meta-recursion design choices for each run?

Q161: JSONL content structure: Examine a sample from baseline_run2.jsonl file. Is structure: [raw conversation turns]? Or [turns with embedded tensor outputs]? If tensors embedded, can parse directly without re-projection (saves time).

Q162: Specification of 'compact_trim': Search experiment directories and git history. What exactly was trimmed or compacted in long_compact_trim_run1? Which meta-recursion components were removed? Critical for interpreting results.

Q163: Projector performance assessment: Once baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim projections complete, measure wall-clock time taken. Extrapolate: if 100-turn baseline takes 15 minutes, full archive (14 runs, ~600 turns) would take ~90 minutes. Is this acceptable? Or does time cost favor FALLBACK B (prospective)?

Q164: N=15 assumption validity: If baseline_run2 pattern is clear/stable at turn 15, can we trust that N=15 sampling works across all runs? Or should we project one longer sample (e.g., long_compact_trim_run1 at N=40) to validate assumption robustness?

Q165: Test_author projection priority: Should cycle 61 dispatch test_author_run1 immediately after baseline/compact_trim complete (turn 185-186)? Or wait for full analysis of baseline vs. compact_trim before testing decoder closure?

Q166: Decoder closure success threshold: If test_author external model achieves >80% comprehension of baseline tensors, is that 'success'? What threshold determines that baseline tensor output is high-quality enough for H2 hypothesis confidence?

Q167: If decoder closure FAILS for baseline tensors (external model cannot understand them), what does that imply? Does it invalidate the entire archive analysis? Or can we still learn from patterns in baseline vs. variant comparisons (even if external validation failed)?

Q168: Input comparability gate: If metadata investigation shows baseline_run1 and baseline_run2 used DIFFERENT input conversations, can we still meaningfully compare epistemic patterns? Or must we identify a matched pair (baseline_run_X vs. compact_trim variant_X, using same input)?

Q169: Redundant projections reconciliation: Cycle 60's baseline_run2 projection and cycle 61's baseline_run2 projection—what is the status of BOTH? Have they completed, or are both running? Should cycle 61 consolidate outputs or cancel duplicate?

Q170: Decision criteria for FRONT vs. FALLBACK: What is the explicit decision rule? Example criteria: 'Proceed with FRONT if (1) both projections complete by turn 184, (2) metadata shows inputs comparable, (3) projector speed <20 min per 100-turn session, (4) decoder closure success >75%.' Establish criteria before turn 184 decision point.

Q171: If projection timeouts occur in turn 184, what is immediate response? (a) Retry with N=10? (b) Optimize projector and retry? (c) Immediately pivot to FALLBACK B? How much time is spent on (a)/(b) before (c) is triggered?

Q172: Confidence labeling protocol: How will cycle 61 distinguish CONFIDENT vs. PROVISIONAL vs. EXPLORATORY findings? Example: 'Baseline shows stable truth=0.85 (CONFIDENT, based on N=15 projection)' vs. 'Compact_trim may show lower truth (PROVISIONAL, pending full projection)' vs. 'Initial pattern suggests meta-recursion matters (EXPLORATORY, hypothesis to test).'

Q173: Information loss mitigation for FALLBACK C (shallow analysis): If cycle 61 recommends FALLBACK C, what is the protocol? Project N=5-10 across all 14 runs; extract candidate patterns; label as EXPLORATORY; use findings to design targeted prospective validation experiments for cycles 62-64?

Q174: Archive analysis time budget: What is acceptable timeline for FRONT PATH (retrospective)? If projections are slow and full analysis takes 2-3 cycles (cycles 61-63), is this still preferred over FALLBACK B (prospective, estimated 3-4 cycles)? Time comparison needed for path selection.

Q175: Contingency planning: Should cycle 61 draft FALLBACK B (prospective experiment design) in parallel with archive analysis, so that if archive path fails, prospective design is ready for immediate execution in cycle 62?

Q176: Feasibility report format: How should cycle 61 communicate its feasibility assessment and FRONT vs. FALLBACK recommendation to cycles 62-65? Explicit decision matrix? Ranked recommendations? Clear statement of blockers and risk?

Q177: If both projections complete successfully but metadata remains opaque, does cycle 61 proceed with FRONT PATH (analysis) or recommend FALLBACK due to metadata risk? What is the risk tolerance for proceeding without full experimental context?

Q178: Retrospective vs. prospective efficiency: If archive analysis shows H2 is supported (meta-recursion essential), can cycle 63 confidently design optimization experiments (cycle 55 per-batch/gating)? Or is FALLBACK C outcome (archive-informed, prospective-validated) required for confidence in design decision?
instructions_for_next:
CYCLE 61-62 TRANSITION PROTOCOL: PROJECTION COMPLETION AND FEASIBILITY ASSESSMENT

=== CYCLE 61 IMMEDIATE ACTIONS (Turns 183-185) ===

PRIORITY 1 (Turn 183-184): PROJECTION STATUS POLLING
  (1) Query background task status for b5mvz85yy (cycle 60 baseline_run2) and bnk191til (cycle 61 long_compact_trim).
  (2) Check output directories:
      - /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/baseline_run2/ (should contain tensor cycle outputs)
      - /home/tony/projects/hamutay/experiments/long_compact_trim/ (should contain compact_trim tensor outputs)
  (3) If outputs found: Parse immediately. Extract epistemic trajectories (truth/indeterminacy/falsity for turns 1-15), strand inventories, declared losses.
  (4) If outputs NOT found by turn 184: Declare PROJECTION TIMEOUT. Escalate to FALLBACK assessment.

PRIORITY 1B (Turn 184 PARALLEL): METADATA INVESTIGATION
  While awaiting projection outputs, conduct parallel investigation:
  (1) JSONL HEADER EXAMINATION (10 min max):
      - Examine first 10 lines of baseline_run1.jsonl, baseline_run2.jsonl, baseline_run3.jsonl
      - Check for: input conversation hash, version tag, date, parameters
      - Question: Are headers identical (same input) or different?
  (2) GIT HISTORY AUDIT (10 min max):
      - `git log --oneline | grep -E 'baseline|projector|version'` (identify relevant commits)
      - `git tag -l | grep -E 'v0\.|cycle'` (list version tags)
      - Assign projector version to each archived run based on creation date
  (3) FILE TIMESTAMP INSPECTION (5 min max):
      - `ls -la /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/*/` (check directory timestamps)
      - Do baselines cluster together, or spread across time? Suggests input similarity or variation.
  (4) EXPERIMENT DOCUMENTATION SEARCH (10 min max):
      - Locate README, config.json, or notes files in experiment directories
      - Search for explicit documentation of experimental purpose, parameters, meta-recursion design

  OUTPUT BY TURN 185: METADATA TABLE
    Run Name | Input Hash | Version | Date | Turns | Size | Experimental Purpose | Comparability
    [populate from investigation; mark unknowns as '?']
    
  COMPARABILITY ASSESSMENT: Are baseline runs comparable? (YES if same input + same version / NO if different / PARTIAL if input same but version different)

PRIORITY 2 (Turn 184): N=15 ASSUMPTION TESTING
  Once baseline_run2 projection is available:
  (1) Extract epistemic trajectory: truth values for turns 1, 3, 5, 7, 10, 15.
  (2) Plot or examine sequence. Questions:
      - Do values show STABLE TREND (e.g., truth gradually increases or stabilizes)? → Assumption HOLDS
      - Do values show HIGH VARIANCE (e.g., truth jumps erratically)? → Assumption FAILS; need longer sample
      - Do values show CLEAR PATTERN (e.g., converging to truth≈0.8)? → Assumption strong
      - Do values show NO CLEAR PATTERN (e.g., oscillating)? → Assumption weak
  (3) Explicit finding: 'N=15 assumption [HELD/FAILED] for baseline_run2. Pattern clarity: [HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW].'

PRIORITY 3 (Turn 185, IF projections completed): BASELINE vs. COMPACT_TRIM COMPARISON
  If both baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim projections are available:
  (1) Extract comparative metrics:
      - Baseline (turns 1-15): truth, indeterminacy, falsity; strand count; declared losses
      - Compact_trim (turns 1-15): same metrics
      - Differences: ΔTruth = baseline_truth - compact_trim_truth, ΔLosses, ΔStrand_count
  (2) Preliminary H2 assessment:
      - If ΔTruth is negative (compact_trim has LOWER truth): Supports H2 (meta-recursion essential)
      - If ΔTruth ≈ 0 (similar truth): Refutes H2 (no degradation from recursion reduction)
      - If pattern is unclear: EXPLORATORY finding; requires full projection for confidence
  (3) Label finding as PROVISIONAL (based on N=15 sampling) or CONFIDENT (if pattern is strong and consistent)

PRIORITY 4 (Turn 185): FEASIBILITY GATE DECISION
  Based on (1)-(3) above, cycle 61 produces FEASIBILITY REPORT:
  
  GREEN GATE (proceed with FRONT PATH):
    ✓ Both projections completed successfully
    ✓ Metadata shows baseline runs are comparable (same input or well-documented variation)
    ✓ N=15 assumption holds (epistemic pattern is clear)
    ✓ Projector performance is acceptable (<15 min per 100-turn session)
    ACTION: Proceed to cycle 62 for full retrospective analysis (project test_author, full variants, conduct H1-H4 hypothesis testing)
    TIMELINE: Cycles 62-63 hypothesis testing, cycle 64 design decision
  
  YELLOW GATE (proceed with FALLBACK C - SHALLOW ANALYSIS):
    ✓ Projections completed, but SLOWLY (>15 min per 100-turn)
    ◐ Metadata is PARTIALLY opaque (some runs unclear purpose/version)
    ◐ N=15 assumption WEAKLY holds (pattern is present but noisy)
    ACTION: Conduct shallow archive analysis (project N=5-10 across multiple runs); identify candidate patterns; label as EXPLORATORY; prepare targeted prospective validation for cycles 62-64
    TIMELINE: Cycles 61-62 shallow analysis, cycles 63-64 prospective validation
  
  RED GATE (proceed with FALLBACK B - PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENTS):
    ✗ Projections timed out or errored
    ✗ Metadata shows archive is INCOMPARABLE (different inputs, different versions, purpose unclear)
    ✗ N=15 assumption FAILS (no clear epistemic pattern)
    ✗ Projector performance is UNACCEPTABLE (>30 min per 100-turn session, or optimization needed)
    ACTION: Abandon retrospective archive analysis. Design CLEAN PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENTS for cycles 62-64. (Same input, single projector version, systematic baseline vs. compact_trim vs. optimized variants)
    TIMELINE: Cycles 62-64 prospective execution, cycle 65 analysis and design decision
  
  GATE OUTPUT BY TURN 185: Explicit recommendation ('FRONT PATH viable with HIGH confidence' / 'FALLBACK C recommended due to [reason]' / 'FALLBACK B required due to [reason]')

PRIORITY 5 (Turn 185, if FRONT PATH gate opens): QUEUE NEXT PROJECTIONS
  (1) Dispatch test_author_run1 projection (55 turns, N=15 or N=20 for richer decoder closure results)
  (2) Optionally dispatch test_author_run2 and test_author_run3 (for robustness validation)
  (3) Expected completion: turns 186-187

PRIORITY 6 (Turn 185, if FALLBACK C opens): PREPARE SHALLOW ANALYSIS PROTOCOL
  (1) Design sampling strategy: N=5-10 per run, covering baseline, compact_trim, and 1-2 other variants
  (2) Identify CANDIDATE PATTERNS to extract: strand count, meta/domain ratio, declared losses, truth trend
  (3) Label all findings as EXPLORATORY
  (4) Prepare prospective validation plan for cycles 63-64

PRIORITY 7 (Turn 185, if FALLBACK B opens): DRAFT PROSPECTIVE EXPERIMENT DESIGN
  (1) Design CONTROLLED COMPARISON: baseline vs. compact_trim vs. [optional 3rd variant]
  (2) Specification: single input conversation, single projector version, systematic variant execution
  (3) Hypothesis map: Which variant comparison tests H1? H2? H3? H4?
  (4) Expected cycles: 62-64 execution, 65 analysis
  (5) Present design to Tony for approval by cycle 62 start

=== CYCLE 62 PROTOCOL (Contingent on Cycle 61 Gate Decision) ===

IF FRONT PATH (green gate):
  • Parse test_author projections (turn 186-187)
  • Validate decoder closure: Did external model understand baseline and compact_trim tensors?
  • Conduct full H1-H4 hypothesis testing using baseline vs. variants
  • Generate hypothesis posteriors: P(H1), P(H2), P(H3), P(H4)
  • Recommendation for cycle 64 design decision ready by cycle 63

IF FALLBACK C (yellow gate):
  • Execute shallow archive sampling (N=5-10 across multiple runs)
  • Extract candidate patterns
  • Document all findings as EXPLORATORY with explicit uncertainty
  • Design targeted prospective validation experiments
  • Plan: cycles 62-63 validation execution, cycle 64 analysis

IF FALLBACK B (red gate):
  • Finalize prospective experiment design
  • Execute baseline vs. compact_trim comparison (same input, controlled conditions)
  • Analyze results
  • Generate hypothesis posteriors based on fresh prospective data
  • Recommendation for cycle 65 design decision

=== CRITICAL DISCIPLINE FOR CYCLES 61-62 ===

1. DECLARE LOSSES OPENLY: Every projection timeout, every metadata gap, every assumption failure is EXPLICITLY noted. No silent loss.
2. TEST ASSUMPTIONS: N=15 assumption is tested before trusting it. Metadata comparability is verified before claiming archive is valid.
3. MAINTAIN PIVOT OPTION: If archive analysis becomes infeasible (projections slow, metadata opaque, assumptions fail), cycle 61 CHANGES STRATEGY rather than forcing bad data into hypotheses.
4. LABEL FINDINGS BY CONFIDENCE: CONFIDENT (full data), PROVISIONAL (partial data), EXPLORATORY (limited data). Readers know what basis each finding rests on.
5. RESPECT THE GATE: The feasibility gate at turn 185 is binding. If RED or YELLOW gate opens, cycle 62 executes corresponding FALLBACK, not FRONT PATH.

=== SUCCESS CRITERIA FOR CYCLE 61 ===

Cycle 61 is successful if it produces:
  1. PROJECTION STATUS REPORT: Both baseline_run2 and long_compact_trim outputs present or explicitly declared missing (with timeout reason)
  2. METADATA ASSESSMENT: Table showing input comparability, projector version, experimental purpose for key runs
  3. ASSUMPTION TEST RESULT: N=15 assumption explicitly tested; HELD or FAILED with evidence
  4. PRELIMINARY H2 FINDINGS: Baseline vs. compact_trim comparison showing direction and magnitude of differences (PROVISIONAL, pending validation)
  5. FEASIBILITY GATE: Explicit recommendation (FRONT / FALLBACK C / FALLBACK B) with justification and blockers listed
  6. NEXT CYCLE PROTOCOL: Clear instructions for cycle 62 contingent on gate decision
  7. UPDATED DECLARED LOSSES: All information gaps, unresolved questions, and assumptions explicitly listed

If cycle 61 delivers all seven items by turn 185, the retrospective vs. prospective path is resolved, and cycles 62-65 can proceed with confidence.
